title: The record was corrected by other labs replicating, never by a lone reader's audit — so is the real frontier defense not internal scrutiny at all but independent corroboration, harder to fake than reputation and not reducible to "what others say"?
summary: One witness can be coached; two who never met cannot rehearse the same lie — yet the courtroom still needs a reader to catch the forged signature.

sources.label: Independent only counts without a common cause; copies of one source weaken support (PMC7670039)
Reproducibility Project — only ~36% of 100 studies replicated (Wikipedia)
Schön caught by identical noise across graphs, by a reader not a replication (Science)
Stapel exposed by data audit; failed replications were blamed on skill (Wikipedia)
The honesty-study fabrication found because the data were posted (Data Colada)
A replication failure has three readings — it is ambiguous evidence (NIH Bookshelf)
Replication is expensive and structurally disincentivized ($0.5–2M) (Castor)
Diagnostic only in low-uncertainty fields; else organic, uncorrected forgetting (Peterson & Panofsky)
Science is not necessarily self-correcting; replications rare and undervalued (Ioannidis)
Multiple attestation values independent access to reality, not a shared tradition (Themelios)
Triangulate distinct, independent evidence types; cognitively demanding (DiverseDaily)
Corroboration is the harder-to-fake backstop, but not automatic (PMC11444786)
